text: NALTOMA AI operates under a fully remote-first policy. All engineers and researchers may work from any location, provided they are available during core hours from 10:00 to 16:00 JST on weekdays. Each team holds a mandatory synchronous stand-up of no more than 15 minutes each morning. Employees who prefer an office environment may use the Tokyo co-working space, which requires a desk reservation at least 24 hours in advance through the internal portal.

Business expenses must be submitted via the expense portal within 30 calendar days of purchase. Client meals are reimbursable up to 5,000 JPY per person per event. Travel by bullet train is approved for trips over 100 km; flights require prior approval from a department head. Receipts are mandatory for any expense exceeding 1,000 JPY. Reimbursements are processed in the monthly payroll cycle on the last business day of each month.

All pull requests to the main branch require a minimum of two approvals from team members who were not the PR author. Reviewers are expected to respond within one business day. PRs must include unit tests achieving at least 80 percent coverage of new code. Any PR that reduces overall test coverage below 75 percent will be automatically blocked by the CI pipeline. Large PRs exceeding 400 lines of diff should be split into smaller, reviewable units.

Production deployments at NALTOMA AI are scheduled on Tuesdays and Thursdays at 02:00 JST to minimize user impact. Each deployment requires an approved change ticket created at least 48 hours in advance. A designated on-call engineer must remain available for two hours after every deployment. Rollback procedures must be documented in the change ticket before approval is granted. Emergency hotfix deployments may occur at any time but require CTO sign-off.

NALTOMA AI maintains four conference rooms labeled A through D at the Tokyo office. Rooms A and B accommodate up to 8 people; rooms C and D hold up to 20 people. Bookings are made through the internal calendar and are limited to two hours per slot per team per day. Rooms unoccupied 10 minutes after the start time are automatically released. External guests must be registered at reception at least one hour before the scheduled meeting.

The NALTOMA AI internal inference API enforces a rate limit of 120 requests per minute per team token. A burst allowance of up to 300 requests per minute is permitted for periods not exceeding 20 seconds. Requests that exceed the limit receive a 429 status code with a Retry-After header indicating when to retry. Teams requiring higher sustained throughput must submit a capacity request to the platform team at least two weeks in advance. All API usage is logged for billing and compliance purposes.

New NALTOMA AI employees begin with a structured two-week onboarding program. During the first week, new hires complete mandatory security and compliance training and are assigned a buddy from their team. Hardware — a MacBook Pro and peripherals — is shipped before the start date. Access to production systems is granted only after security training is marked complete in the HR system. The second week focuses on team-specific tooling and a guided walkthrough of current projects.

Performance reviews at NALTOMA AI are conducted twice per year, in June and December. The process begins with a self-assessment using a five-point scale across four dimensions: technical impact, collaboration, communication, and growth mindset. Managers submit evaluations within two weeks of the self-assessment deadline. Compensation adjustments resulting from reviews take effect on the first day of the following month. Employees rated below 2 in any dimension are placed on a 60-day improvement plan.
